{{Quote|They know it’s coming, and they’re turning to face it. Khaddor isn’t trying to warn anyone about the cost of surrendering to darkness. He’s talking about how you meet it. You turn and look into it.|Han Solo interpreting the painting|<ref name="TG" />}}
'''''Killik Twilight''''' was an [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Moss painting/Legends|moss painting]] by [[Ob Khaddor]] that depicted the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] either turning away from the oncoming destruction of their [[Species/Legends|species]] or turning to meet it and face their conqueror. The [[Painting/Legends|painting]] also held a hidden [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Shadowcast]] keycode. It was on its way back to Alderaan, being borrowed for an art exhibit, when the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was destroyed by the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. The painting was one of the last of its kind and could not be replicated since it was made with moss that only grew on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]].

==History==
In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], the painting was [[Mission to Tatooine (Killik Twilight)|stolen]] by [[Kitster Chanchani Banai]] during an auction on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="TG" /> Later it passed through several hands, including the Solos and the [[Squib/Legends|Squibs]] [[Emala]], [[Grees]], and [[Sligh]], before eventually ending up with the crew of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']]. Fortunately for the New Republic, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] were able to recover the keycode first. Many years later, [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] (who had come into possession of the painting through Grand Admiral [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]]) gave it to Han and Leia after the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|long war]] with the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].

The painting also needed to be watered constantly or else the moss would dry up.
